questions.q: Which ministerial office did Maulana Abul Kalam Azad hold as independent India's first?
questions.explanation: He was independent India's first Minister of Education.

questions.q: On which date is National Education Day observed in honour of Maulana Azad?
questions.explanation: His birthday, 11 November, is celebrated as National Education Day.

questions.q: Which observance marks Maulana Azad's birthday?
questions.explanation: His birthday is celebrated as National Education Day.

questions.q: In which year did Maulana Azad become India's first Education Minister?
questions.explanation: He took charge as Education Minister in 1947.

questions.q: Until which year did Maulana Azad serve as Education Minister?
questions.explanation: He served as Education Minister from 1947 to 1958.

questions.q: Maulana Azad served in the first cabinet under which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He served as Education Minister in Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru's cabinet.

questions.q: The honorific 'Maulana' in Maulana Azad's name denotes him as which of the following?
questions.explanation: 'Maulana' is an honorific for a learned religious scholar.

questions.q: Maulana Azad's portfolio as a Union minister was concerned with which sector?
questions.explanation: He headed the education sector as the first Education Minister.

questions.q: How many years did Maulana Azad serve as Education Minister?
questions.explanation: He served from 1947 to 1958, about eleven years.

questions.q: Maulana Azad was a senior leader of which organisation during the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: He was a senior leader of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: Maulana Azad is associated with the founding of which body during his tenure?
questions.explanation: The University Grants Commission was established during his tenure as Education Minister.

questions.q: The first Indian Institute of Technology was established during whose tenure as Education Minister?
questions.explanation: The first IIT, at Kharagpur, was set up during Azad's tenure as Education Minister.

questions.q: What is the full name reflected in the card's title for Maulana Azad?
questions.explanation: His full name was Maulana Abul Kalam Azad.

questions.q: Maulana Azad presided over the Indian National Congress during which broad period?
questions.explanation: He was Congress President through the early 1940s, including the Quit India period.

questions.q: Maulana Azad's memoir of the freedom struggle is titled what?
questions.explanation: His memoir is titled 'India Wins Freedom'.

questions.q: As Education Minister, Maulana Azad emphasised free and compulsory education for which group?
options: Children up to a certain age
questions.explanation: He stressed universal free and compulsory education for children.

questions.q: Which body promoting cultural relations was set up during Maulana Azad's tenure as Education Minister?
options: The Indian Council for Cultural Relations
questions.explanation: The Indian Council for Cultural Relations was founded during his tenure.

questions.q: In which year did Maulana Azad pass away while still serving as Education Minister?
questions.explanation: He died in 1958, while serving as Education Minister.

questions.q: Maulana Azad's role placed him in charge of which ministry of the Union government?
questions.explanation: He headed the Ministry of Education as its first minister.

questions.q: Maulana Azad was known in the freedom movement as a proponent of which idea between communities?
questions.explanation: He was an advocate of composite, inclusive nationalism uniting communities.

questions.q: Maulana Azad is regarded as having laid the foundations of India's framework in which field of governance?
questions.explanation: As first Education Minister, he is credited with shaping India's national education framework.

questions.q: The national academies for letters, music and the arts set up during Maulana Azad's tenure served which sector?
questions.explanation: The national academies for letters, music and the arts were established under his education ministry, serving culture and the arts.

questions.q: In which year was Maulana Azad born, the date now observed as National Education Day?
questions.explanation: He was born on 11 November 1888, observed as National Education Day.

questions.q: Maulana Azad edited which influential Urdu weekly journal during the freedom movement?
questions.explanation: He founded and edited the Urdu weekly 'Al-Hilal'.

questions.q: In which year was Maulana Azad posthumously conferred the Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Bharat Ratna posthumously in 1992.

questions.q: The University Grants Commission, established during Azad's tenure, was formally constituted by statute in which year?
questions.explanation: The UGC was established as a statutory body in 1956 during his tenure.

questions.q: Maulana Azad was elected President of the Indian National Congress for a long term beginning at which 1940 session?
questions.explanation: He was elected Congress President at the 1940 Ramgarh session.

questions.q: Which national institute of technology is named after Maulana Azad?
options: Maulana Azad National Institute of Technology
questions.explanation: The Maulana Azad National Institute of Technology is named in his honour.

questions.q: Maulana Azad served as Congress President continuously through which key wartime years of the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: He served as Congress President from 1940 to 1946, spanning the Quit India and pre-independence period.

questions.q: National academies for letters, music, drama and the visual arts were established during whose tenure as Education Minister?
questions.explanation: The national cultural academies were set up during Azad's tenure as Education Minister.
